pkg.name: angular
pkg.description:
The Nx Plugin for Angular contains executors, generators, and utilities for managing Angular applications and libraries within an Nx workspace. It provides: 

- Integration with libraries such as Storybook, Jest, ESLint, Tailwind CSS, Playwright and Cypress. 

- Generators to help scaffold code quickly (like: Micro Frontends, Libraries, both internal to your codebase and publishable to npm) 

- Single Component Application Modules (SCAMs) 

- NgRx helpers. 

- Utilities for automatic workspace refactoring.

/nx-api/angular/documents/overview.name: Overview
/nx-api/angular/documents/overview.description:
The Nx Plugin for Angular contains executors, generators, and utilities for managing Angular applications and libraries within an Nx workspace. It provides: 

- Integration with libraries such as Storybook, Jest, ESLint, Tailwind CSS, Playwright and Cypress. 

- Generators to help scaffold code quickly (like: Micro Frontends, Libraries, both internal to your codebase and publishable to npm) 

- Single Component Application Modules (SCAMs) 

- NgRx helpers. 

- Utilities for automatic workspace refactoring.

/nx-api/angular/documents/nx-and-angular.name: Nx and the Angular CLI
/nx-api/angular/documents/nx-and-angular.description:

/nx-api/angular/documents/angular-nx-ver.name: Angular and Nx Version Matrix
/nx-api/angular/documents/angular-nx-ver.description:
The Nx Plugin for Angular contains executors, generators, and utilities for managing Angular applications and libraries within an Nx workspace. It provides: 

- Integration with libraries such as Storybook, Jest, ESLint, Tailwind CSS, Playwright and Cypress.

/nx-api/angular/executors/delegate-build.description: Delegates the build to a different target while supporting incremental builds.
/nx-api/angular/executors/delegate-build.name: delegate-build

/nx-api/angular/executors/ng-packagr-lit.description:
Builds an Angular library with support for incremental builds.

This executor is meant to be used with buildable libraries in an incremental build scenario. It is similar to the `@nx/angular:package` executor but it only produces ESM2022 bundles.
/nx-api/angular/executors/ng-packagr-lit.name: ng-packagr-lite

/nx-api/angular/executors/package.description:
Builds and packages an Angular library producing an output following the Angular Package Format (APF) to be distributed as an NPM package.

This executor is a drop-in replacement for the `@angular-devkit/build-angular:ng-packagr` and `@angular/build:ng-packagr` builders, with additional support for incremental builds.
/nx-api/angular/executors/package.name: package

/nx-api/angular/executors/module-federat.description: Serves host [Module Federation](https://module-federation.io/) applications ([webpack](https://webpack.js.org/)-based) allowing to specify which remote applications should be served with the host.
/nx-api/angular/executors/module-federat.name: module-federation-dev-server

/nx-api/angular/executors/module-federat.description: The module-federation-ssr-dev-server executor is reserved exclusively for use with host SSR Module Federation applications. It allows the user to specify which remote applications should be served with the host.
/nx-api/angular/executors/module-federat.name: module-federation-dev-ssr

/nx-api/angular/executors/application.description: Builds an Angular application using [esbuild](https://esbuild.github.io/) with integrated SSR and prerendering capabilities.
/nx-api/angular/executors/application.name: application

/nx-api/angular/executors/extract-i18n.description: Extracts i18n messages from source code.
/nx-api/angular/executors/extract-i18n.name: extract-i18n

/nx-api/angular/executors/dev-server.description: Serves an Angular application using [webpack](https://webpack.js.org/) when the build target is using a webpack-based executor, or [Vite](https://vitejs.dev/) when the build target uses an [esbuild](https://esbuild.github.io/)-based executor.
/nx-api/angular/executors/dev-server.name: dev-server

/nx-api/angular/executors/webpack-server.description: Builds a server Angular application using [webpack](https://webpack.js.org/). This executor is a drop-in replacement for the `@angular-devkit/build-angular:server` builder provided by the Angular CLI. It is usually used in tandem with the `@nx/angular:webpack-browser` executor when your Angular application uses a custom webpack configuration.
/nx-api/angular/executors/webpack-server.name: webpack-server

/nx-api/angular/generators/add-linting.description: Adds linting configuration to an Angular project.
/nx-api/angular/generators/add-linting.name: add-linting

/nx-api/angular/generators/component-sto.description: Creates a stories.ts file for a component.
/nx-api/angular/generators/component-sto.name: component-story

/nx-api/angular/generators/component-tes.description: Creates a cypress component test file for a component.
/nx-api/angular/generators/component-tes.name: component-test

/nx-api/angular/generators/convert-to-ap.description: Converts projects to use the `@nx/angular:application` executor or the `@angular-devkit/build-angular:application` builder.
/nx-api/angular/generators/convert-to-ap.name: convert-to-application-executor

/nx-api/angular/generators/convert-to-rs.description: Converts Angular Webpack projects to use Rspack.
/nx-api/angular/generators/convert-to-rs.name: convert-to-rspack

/nx-api/angular/generators/federate-modu.description: Create a federated module, which is exposed by a remote and can be subsequently loaded by a host.
/nx-api/angular/generators/federate-modu.name: federate-module

/nx-api/angular/generators/library-secon.description: Creates a secondary entry point for an Angular publishable library.
/nx-api/angular/generators/library-secon.name: library-secondary-entry-point

/nx-api/angular/generators/remote.description: Generate a Remote Angular Module Federation Application.
/nx-api/angular/generators/remote.name: remote

/nx-api/angular/generators/move.description: Moves an Angular application or library to another folder within the workspace and updates the project configuration.
/nx-api/angular/generators/move.name: move

/nx-api/angular/generators/convert-to-wi.description:
Converts an old micro frontend configuration to use the new withModuleFederation helper. It will run successfully if the following conditions are met: 
 - Is either a host or remote application 
 - Shared npm package configurations have not been modified 
 - Name used to identify the Micro Frontend application matches the project name 

{% callout type="warning" title="Overrides" %}This generator will overwrite your webpack config. If you have additional custom configuration in your config file, it will be lost!{% /callout %}
/nx-api/angular/generators/convert-to-wi.name: convert-to-with-mf

/nx-api/angular/generators/host.description: Generate a Host Angular Module Federation Application.
/nx-api/angular/generators/host.name: host

/nx-api/angular/generators/ng-add.description: Migrates an Angular CLI workspace to Nx or adds the Angular plugin to an Nx workspace.
/nx-api/angular/generators/ng-add.name: ng-add

/nx-api/angular/generators/ngrx.description: Adds NgRx support to an application or library.
/nx-api/angular/generators/ngrx.name: ngrx

/nx-api/angular/generators/ngrx-feature-.description: Adds an NgRx Feature Store to an application or library.
/nx-api/angular/generators/ngrx-feature-.name: ngrx-feature-store

/nx-api/angular/generators/ngrx-root-sto.description: Adds an NgRx Root Store to an application.
/nx-api/angular/generators/ngrx-root-sto.name: ngrx-root-store

/nx-api/angular/generators/scam-to-stand.description: Convert an existing Single Component Angular Module (SCAM) to a Standalone Component.
/nx-api/angular/generators/scam-to-stand.name: scam-to-standalone

/nx-api/angular/generators/scam.description: Generate a component with an accompanying Single Component Angular Module (SCAM).
/nx-api/angular/generators/scam.name: scam

/nx-api/angular/generators/scam-directiv.description: Generate a directive with an accompanying Single Component Angular Module (SCAM).
/nx-api/angular/generators/scam-directiv.name: scam-directive

/nx-api/angular/generators/scam-pipe.description: Generate a pipe with an accompanying Single Component Angular Module (SCAM).
/nx-api/angular/generators/scam-pipe.name: scam-pipe

/nx-api/angular/generators/setup-mf.description: Generate a Module Federation configuration for a given Angular application.
/nx-api/angular/generators/setup-mf.name: setup-mf

/nx-api/angular/generators/setup-ssr.description: Generate Angular Universal (SSR) setup for an Angular application.
/nx-api/angular/generators/setup-ssr.name: setup-ssr

/nx-api/angular/generators/setup-tailwin.description: Configures Tailwind CSS for an application or a buildable/publishable library.
/nx-api/angular/generators/setup-tailwin.name: setup-tailwind

/nx-api/angular/generators/stories.description: Creates stories/specs for all components declared in a project.
/nx-api/angular/generators/stories.name: stories

/nx-api/angular/generators/storybook-con.description: Adds Storybook configuration to a project.
/nx-api/angular/generators/storybook-con.name: storybook-configuration

/nx-api/angular/generators/cypress-compo.description: Setup Cypress component testing for a project.
/nx-api/angular/generators/cypress-compo.name: cypress-component-configuration

/nx-api/angular/migrations/ensure-nx-mod.description: If workspace includes Module Federation projects, ensure the new @nx/module-federation package is installed.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/ensure-nx-mod.name: ensure-nx-module-federation-package

/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-angula.description: Remove Angular ESLint rules that were removed in v19.0.0.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-angula.name: remove-angular-eslint-rules

/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-tailwi.description: Remove the deprecated 'tailwindConfig' option from ng-packagr executors. Tailwind CSS configurations located at the project or workspace root will be picked up automatically.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-tailwi.name: remove-tailwind-config-from-ng-packagr-executors

/nx-api/angular/migrations/disable-angul.description: Disable the Angular ESLint prefer-standalone rule if not set.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/disable-angul.name: disable-angular-eslint-prefer-standalone

/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-localize-.description: Add the '@angular/localize/init' polyfill to the 'polyfills' option of targets using esbuild-based executors.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-localize-.name: add-localize-polyfill-to-targets

/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-angula.description: Update '@angular/ssr' import paths to use the new '/node' entry point when 'CommonEngine' is detected.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-angula.name: update-angular-ssr-imports-to-use-node-entry-point

/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-19-6-1.description: Ensure Target Defaults are set correctly for Module Federation.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-19-6-1.name: update-19-6-1-ensure-module-federation-target-defaults

/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-19-6-0.description: Ensure Module Federation DTS is turned off by default.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-19-6-0.name: update-19-6-0

/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-typescrip.description: Installs the '@typescript-eslint/utils' package when having installed '@angular-eslint/eslint-plugin' or '@angular-eslint/eslint-plugin-template' with version >=18.0.0.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-typescrip.name: add-typescript-eslint-utils

/nx-api/angular/migrations/fix-target-de.description: Ensure targetDefaults inputs for task hashing when '@nx/angular:webpack-browser' is used are correct for Module Federation.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/fix-target-de.name: fix-target-defaults-for-webpack-browser

/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-module-fe.description: Add NX_MF_DEV_SERVER_STATIC_REMOTES to inputs for task hashing when '@nx/angular:webpack-browser' is used for Module Federation.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-module-fe.name: add-module-federation-env-var-to-target-defaults

/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-browser-s.description: Add 'browser-sync' as dev dependency when '@angular-devkit/build-angular:ssr-dev-server' or '@nx/angular:module-federation-dev-ssr' is used.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-browser-s.name: add-browser-sync-dependency

/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-autoprefi.description: Add 'autoprefixer' as dev dependency when '@nx/angular:ng-packagr-lite' or '@nx/angular:package` is used.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/add-autoprefi.name: add-autoprefixer-dependency

/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-zone-j.description: Replace the deep imports from 'zone.js/dist/zone' and 'zone.js/dist/zone-testing' with 'zone.js' and 'zone.js/testing'.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-zone-j.name: update-zone-js-deep-import

/nx-api/angular/migrations/explicitly-se.description: Explicitly set 'updateBuildableProjectDepsInPackageJson' to 'true' in targets that rely on that value as the default.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/explicitly-se.name: explicitly-set-projects-to-update-buildable-deps

/nx-api/angular/migrations/switch-data-p.description: Switch the data persistence operator imports to '@ngrx/router-store/data-persistence'.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/switch-data-p.name: switch-data-persistence-operators-imports-to-ngrx-router-store

/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-render.description: Remove exported `@angular/platform-server` `renderModule` method. The `renderModule` method is now exported by the Angular CLI.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-render.name: remove-render-module-platform-server-exports

/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-ngcc-i.description: Remove 'ngcc' invocation if exists from the 'postinstall' script in package.json.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-ngcc-i.name: remove-ngcc-invocation

/nx-api/angular/migrations/extract-app-c.description: Extract the app config for standalone apps
/nx-api/angular/migrations/extract-app-c.name: extract-app-config-for-standalone

/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-server.description: Update server executors' configuration to disable 'buildOptimizer' for non optimized builds.
/nx-api/angular/migrations/update-server.name: update-server-executor-config

/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-protra.description: Remove protractor as default e2eTestRunner from nxJson and project configurations
/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-protra.name: remove-protractor-defaults-from-generators

/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-karma-.description: Remove karma as default unitTestRunner from nxJson and project configurations
/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-karma-.name: remove-karma-defaults-from-generators

/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-librar.description: Replace the deprecated library generator 'simpleModuleName' option from generator defaults with 'simpleName'
/nx-api/angular/migrations/remove-librar.name: remove-library-generator-simple-module-name-option

document.content:
---
title: Overview of the Nx Angular Plugin
description: The Nx Plugin for Angular contains executors, generators, and utilities for managing Angular applications and libraries within an Nx workspace.
keywords: [angular]
---

The Nx Plugin for Angular contains executors, generators, and utilities for managing Angular applications and libraries
within an Nx workspace. It also enables using Angular Devkit builders and schematics in Nx workspaces.

Among other things, it provides:

- Integration with libraries such as:
  - Cypress
  - ESLint
  - Jest
  - Playwright
  - Storybook
- Generators to help scaffold code quickly, including:
  - Micro Frontends
  - Libraries, both internal to your codebase and publishable to npm
  - Projects with Tailwind CSS
- Executors providing extra capabilities on top of the Angular Devkit builders:
  - Provide ESBuild plugins
  - Provide custom webpack configurations
- Utilities for automatic workspace refactoring

{% callout type="note" title="Currently using the Angular CLI?" %}
You can easily and mostly **automatically migrate from an Angular CLI** project to Nx! Learn
more [here](/recipes/angular/migration/angular).
{% /callout %}

## Setting Up @nx/angular

### Installation

{% callout type="note" title="Keep Nx Package Versions In Sync" %}
Make sure to install the `@nx/angular` version that matches the version of `nx` in your repository. If the version numbers get out of sync, you can encounter some difficult to debug errors. You can [fix Nx version mismatches with this recipe](/recipes/tips-n-tricks/keep-nx-versions-in-sync).
{% /callout %}

In any Nx workspace, you can install `@nx/angular` by running the following command:

{% tabs %}
{% tab label="Nx 18+" %}

```shell {% skipRescope=true %}
nx add @nx/angular
```

This will install the correct version of `@nx/angular`.

{% /tab %}
{% tab label="Nx < 18" %}

Install the `@nx/angular` package with your package manager.

```shell
npm add -D @nx/angular
```

{% /tab %}
{% /tabs %}

{% callout type="note" title="Angular Tutorial" %}
For a full tutorial experience, follow the [Angular Monorepo Tutorial](/getting-started/tutorials/angular-monorepo-tutorial)
{% /callout %}

## Using the Angular Plugin

### Generating an application

It's straightforward to generate an Angular application:

```shell
nx g @nx/angular:app apps/appName
```

By default, the application will be generated with:

- ESLint as the linter.
- Jest as the unit test runner.
- Cypress as the E2E test runner.

We can then serve, build, test, lint, and run e2e tests on the application with the following commands:

```shell
nx serve appName
nx build appName
nx test appName
nx lint appName
nx e2e appName
```

### Generating a library

Generating an Angular library is very similar to generating an application:

```shell
nx g @nx/angular:lib libs/libName
```

By default, the library will be generated with:

- ESLint as the linter.
- Jest as the unit test runner.

We can then test and lint the library with the following commands:

```shell
nx test libName
nx lint libName
```

Read more about:

- [Creating Libraries](/concepts/decisions/project-size)
- [Library Types](/concepts/decisions/project-dependency-rules)
- [Buildable and Publishable Libraries](/concepts/buildable-and-publishable-libraries)

### Fallback to `@schematics/angular`

If you try to invoke a generator that is not present in `@nx/angular`, the request will automatically be forwarded on
to `@schematics/angular`. So, even though there is no `@nx/angular:service` generator, the following command will
successfully create a service:

```shell
nx g @nx/angular:service apps/appName/src/lib/my-service/my-service
```

## More Documentation

- [Angular Monorepo Tutorial](/getting-started/tutorials/angular-monorepo-tutorial)
- [Migrating from the Angular CLI](/recipes/angular/migration/angular)
- [Setup Module Federation with Angular and Nx](/concepts/module-federation/faster-builds-with-module-federation)
- [Using Tailwind CSS with Angular projects](/recipes/angular/using-tailwind-css-with-angular-projects)
